item.title: Self-Supervised Learning of Lidar Segmentation for Autonomous Indoor Navigation
item.description: My first publication can be found here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.05897. It presents a self-supervised learning approach for semantic segmentation of lidar frames. Basically, the algorithm can identify object “classes” such as walls, long-term movable objects (tables, chairs), and short-term movable objects (people), and this information is used to improve localization and navigation in complex environments. i Big thanks to everyone at the ASRL lab for the opporotunity to work on this probject, especially Tim Barfoot, Hugues Thomas, and Mona Grisdeth.

item.title: What I Wish I Knew In Highschool
item.description: There are some things that I wish my 9th-grade self knew. Here I have collected some of the “wisdom” I have acquired since then. Ignore criticism about things you can’t change This applies to your truly immutable characteristics and the traits that make you you. The former category consists of attributes like height, race, and looks, while the latter includes your passions, personality, and preferences. I don’t mean not to listen to any constructive criticism; tough love can motivate beneficial change.

item.title: Thoughts on AI, Synthetic Biology, and Technological Progress
item.description: Below are a collection of some posts I made to a discussion board 1. While they by no means consitute complete claims, I thought some of the topics and ideas that have been coming up are interesting, so I thought I would share. Contents When to Stop? Substrate Neutrality An Interesting Convergence Transparency in AI systems Philosophical Zombies Anthropomorphism of AI When to Stop? 2021-01-20 The prevailing worldview in western (capitalist) societies is that upward growth is both necessary and inevitable.

item.title: Mind Worms
item.description: I am often afflicted by what I like to call Mind Worms 1: thoughts that I can’t seem to get out of my head, and grow to occupy the majority of my working memory. For example, my2 mind worms often take the form of: a technical problem that I have yet overcome a post-exam obsession/stress over a question I was unable to solve a social or personal anxiety that has been recently aggravated In my experience, mind worms can either
